From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Weight class in combat sports

This article is about the sports concept. For the general concept, see [weight](/wiki/Weight "Weight"). For other uses, see [Lightweight (disambiguation)](/wiki/Lightweight_(disambiguation) "Lightweight (disambiguation)").

**Lightweight** is a [weight class](/wiki/Weight_class "Weight class") in [combat sports](/wiki/Combat_sport "Combat sport") and [rowing](/wiki/Rowing_(sport) "Rowing (sport)").

## Boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Boxing")]

### Professional boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional boxing")]

The lightweight division is over 130 pounds (59 kilograms) and up to 135 pounds (61.2 kilograms) [weight class](/wiki/Boxing_weight_classes "Boxing weight classes") in the sport of [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing").
Notable lightweight boxers include [Henry Armstrong](/wiki/Henry_Armstrong "Henry Armstrong"), [Ken Buchanan](/wiki/Ken_Buchanan "Ken Buchanan"), [Tony Canzoneri](/wiki/Tony_Canzoneri "Tony Canzoneri"), [Pedro Carrasco](/wiki/Pedro_Carrasco "Pedro Carrasco"), [Joel Casamayor](/wiki/Joel_Casamayor "Joel Casamayor"), [Al "Bummy" Davis](/wiki/Al_Davis_(boxer) "Al Davis (boxer)"), [Oscar De La Hoya](/wiki/Oscar_De_La_Hoya "Oscar De La Hoya"), [Roberto Durán](/wiki/Roberto_Dur%C3%A1n "Roberto Durán"), [Joe Gans](/wiki/Joe_Gans "Joe Gans"), [Artur Grigorian](/wiki/Artur_Grigorian "Artur Grigorian"), [Benny Leonard](/wiki/Benny_Leonard "Benny Leonard"), [Ray Mancini](/wiki/Ray_Mancini "Ray Mancini"), [Floyd Mayweather Jr.](/wiki/Floyd_Mayweather_Jr. "Floyd Mayweather Jr."), [Juan Manuel Márquez](/wiki/Juan_Manuel_M%C3%A1rquez "Juan Manuel Márquez"), [Sugar Shane Mosley](/wiki/Sugar_Shane_Mosley "Sugar Shane Mosley"), [Miguel Ángel González](/wiki/Miguel_%C3%81ngel_Gonz%C3%A1lez_(boxer) "Miguel Ángel González (boxer)"), [Carlos Ortiz](/wiki/Carlos_Ortiz_(boxer) "Carlos Ortiz (boxer)"), [Katie Taylor](/wiki/Katie_Taylor "Katie Taylor"), [Edwin Valero](/wiki/Edwin_Valero "Edwin Valero"), [Len Wickwar](/wiki/Len_Wickwar "Len Wickwar"), [Pernell Whitaker](/wiki/Pernell_Whitaker "Pernell Whitaker"), [Manny Pacquiao](/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao "Manny Pacquiao") and [Ike Williams](/wiki/Ike_Williams_(boxer) "Ike Williams (boxer)").

## Kickboxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Kickboxing")]

[International Kickboxing Federation](/wiki/International_Kickboxing_Federation "International Kickboxing Federation") (IKF) Lightweight (Pro & Amateur) 127.1 lb - 132 lb or 57.77 kg - 60 kg.
Women's divisions also use this weight class, but usually at a lower weight than the men's divisions.
In [Glory](/wiki/Glory_(kickboxing) "Glory (kickboxing)") promotion, a lightweight division is up to 70 kg (154 lb).[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In [ONE Championship](/wiki/ONE_Championship "ONE Championship"), the lightweight division limit is 77.1 kg (170 lb).

## Bare-knuckle boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Bare-knuckle boxing")]

The limit for lightweight generally differs among promotions in bare-knuckle boxing:

* In [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"), the lightweight division has an upper limit of 155 lb (70 kg).
* In BKB™, the lightweight division has an upper limit of 79 kg (174 lb).

## Lethwei

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Lethwei")]

In [World Lethwei Championship](/wiki/World_Lethwei_Championship "World Lethwei Championship"), the lightweight division has an upper limit of 60 kg (132 lb).

## Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

Main article: [Lightweight (MMA)](/wiki/Lightweight_(MMA) "Lightweight (MMA)")

In [MMA](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"), the lightweight division is from 146 lb (66 kg) to 155 lb (70 kg).

## Rowing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Rowing")]

Main article: [Lightweight rowing](/wiki/Lightweight_rowing "Lightweight rowing")

At the international level, for crew boats, the limits are:

* Men: Crew average 70 kg (154.3 lb) – no rower over 72.5 kg (159.8 lb)
* Women: Crew average 57 kg (125.7 lb) – no rower over 59 kg (130.1 lb)

For [single sculls](/wiki/Single_scull "Single scull"), the limits are 72.5 kg (159.8 lb) for men and 59 kg (130.1 lb) for women.

Originally, lightweight rowing was an introduced as a category at the [1996 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Rowing_at_the_1996_Summer_Olympics "Rowing at the 1996 Summer Olympics"), due to countries with athletes of smaller average stature competing with an unfair disadvantage, as rowing favors taller athletes who have more leverage.

Current Olympic-class lightweight events in rowing are the men's lightweight double sculls, and the women's lightweight double sculls. From 1996 to 2016, there was also the men's lightweight coxless four event.

## Sumo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lightweight&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Sumo")]

In amateur sumo competitions sanctioned by the [International Sumo Federation](/wiki/International_Sumo_Federation "International Sumo Federation"), the lightweight class is defined as no heavier than 85 kg (187.4 lb) for men, 80 kg (176.4 lb) for boys, 65 kg (143.3 lb) for women, and 60 kg (132.3 lb) for girls.
